data.title: As a developer, my large diff is never rejected solely due to line count; the classifier warns instead
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Modify review-complexity-classifier.sh to emit SIZE_WARNING: <line_count> instead of REVIEW_REJECTED when a diff exceeds 600 lines. Update the classifier-size-output.md contract to replace size_action: reject with size_action: warn. Audit and update all consumers that branch on size_action.

**Why**: The current 600-line hard rejection leaves engineers with no usable review path for large diffs. This change enables the large-commit review path to operate on diffs that would previously be blocked and removes the only workflow-blocking failure mode based on line count alone.

**Scope**:
- IN: classifier change (reject→warn), SIZE_WARNING: <line_count> signal format, classifier-size-output.md contract update, consumer audit covering: REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md Step 3, classifier-telemetry.md size_action enum, review-event-schema.md tier_selection.size_action enum, all test files asserting or branching on size_action=='reject'
- OUT: REVIEW-WORKFLOW-HUGE.md (Story S2), configuration documentation (Story S6)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, staging a commit with >=600 added lines in non-test, non-generated files results in the classifier emitting SIZE_WARNING: <line_count> and size_action: warn in its JSON output; no commit is returned with size_action: reject
  ← Satisfies: "When a diff exceeds 600 lines the classifier appends SIZE_WARNING: <line_count> lines to its output and continues processing; no diff is ever returned with REJECTED status solely due to line count"
- When this story is complete, the classifier-size-output.md contract no longer contains size_action: reject; it defines size_action: warn at the same 600-line threshold; the emitter and parser remain in sync
  ← Satisfies: "The classifier-size-output.md contract is updated to replace size_action: reject with size_action: warn; all consumers branching on size_action are verified to handle the new value before the change ships"
- When this story is complete, no file in the repository references size_action: reject as a valid enum value — this includes classifier-telemetry.md, review-event-schema.md, and all test files that assert or branch on size_action=='reject'
  ← Satisfies: "All consumers branching on size_action are verified to handle the new value before the change ships"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Existing consumer code checking size_action == "reject" will silently pass through on warn — the audit must be exhaustive before shipping to prevent silent behavioral regressions in consumers
- [Testing] SIZE_WARNING behavior must be testable without staging a real 600+ line diff — ensure the classifier's threshold check is unit-testable with injected line counts

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
